name: Neutral Taunt
notes: "Ahhh, time for a break I guess. Hmm? You too little one? Oh, you're such a little nice Monoid. Yes you are, yes you are, yes you are! See ya later!"

name: Forward Taunt
notes: "My power howls! Face me! Just kidding."; if opponent stands directly in front of Ingrid, she performs an attack that deals 0 damage but grants full Super gauge to the opponent on hit/block/parry; references M. Bison's back taunt

name: Back Taunt
notes: "Oop, I forgot I have an urgent matter. You, babysit this one for a while."; summons a controllable Monoid that cannot block and has access to 5 attacks; LP or LK is a divekick-like move that hits low, MP or MK is an overhead, HP or HK is a high strike, HP+HK performs a beam attack, and any button in the air performs an aerial strike; the Monoid is fully controllable mid-air; Ingrid comes back if the Monoid is hit or after not moving for ? frames; when Ingrid returns after standing idle and high fives the Monoid, input LP~LP~4~LK~HK (Modern: L~L~4~M~H) to absorb the Monoid and transform into a more powerful version ("Shin Ingrid"); this transformation also fully heals Ingrid, fills her Super gauge, and grants 4 Sun Crests; Shin Ingrid gains access to 2 new Hidden Art special moves (22PPP and 214214K) and a new SA2 (360+KK)

name: Big Laser?
notes: Shin Ingrid only; tall 16-hit beam projectile that reaches fullscreen; expands forward during the first 4 active frames before reaching full range on the 5th active frame; most/all hits will connect even if opponent falls into the late active frames; chip damage deals 50% of its base damage rather than the usual 25%

name: Burnout Attack?
notes: Shin Ingrid only; immediately puts the opponent into Burnout on hit; cannot be canceled into from normals; hits twice on hit or once on block

name: Sun Octopus?
notes: Shin Ingrid only; command grab Super; opponent cannot jump after the freeze; can be comboed into on grounded opponents; cinematic time regenerates ~1.6 Drive bars for Ingrid

name: Monoid Low Dive
notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; 3 hits (all hit low despite the divekick-like animation); can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; Modern Controls: L

name: Monoid Overhead Hammer
notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; Modern Controls: M

name: Monoid Star Thrust
notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; Modern Controls: H

name: Monoid Beam
notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; cannot steer the attack forward like the other Monoid attacks; knocks down on both grounded and airborne opponents; input has no kara-cancel leniency (must hit HP+HK on the exact same frame); Modern Controls: Sp

name: Monoid Aerial Strike
notes: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; gains a second hitbox on active frames 3-6 that can hit cross-up; Modern Controls: j.X

name: Monoid Super
notes: Slow 3-bar Super with low damage relative to its meter cost; unsafe on hit or block; on hit, Ingrid returns and high-fives the Monoid (can transform into Shin Ingrid using the LP~LP~4~LK~HK input before the high five, becoming much more unsafe on hit); Modern Controls: 214236H
